title: Bilingual-language-improvement measurement strand not produced or enforced (distinct from the Bilingual Communicators SLO coverage)
description: sim-revised-frame.md establishes always-bilingual language improvement as a distinct MEASURED structural strand (a staff English-usage measure + a student bilingual-progress measure on the spiral cadence; R-0009: 'account for always-bilingual language improvement' + 'bilingual dual-purpose framing'). This is DISTINCT from R7 coverage (which only ensures the 'Bilingual Communicators' SLO is targeted-or-gapped). The T5 audit found the bilingual MEASUREMENT strand absent from the assembled draft (no staff English-usage survey, no bilingual student-progress measure, no bilingual feedback channel -- only incidental mission-quote mentions) AND absent from all enforcement (no generation-contract self-check, no check_draft scan). Same class as the three-view parent-track gap (FGAP-037): a stated measurement strand produced for only some tracks, ungated.

evidence.reference: the bilingual 9th-measured-strand + always-bilingual discipline (staff English-usage + student progress on the spiral cadence)
R-0009: always-bilingual language improvement + bilingual dual-purpose framing
no bilingual measurement (no staff English-usage survey / bilingual progress measure / bilingual feedback channel); grep bilingual/chinese/english over check_draft.py + self_check.py returns nothing

impact: Generated plans can omit the bilingual-language-improvement measurement strand while appearing complete; a validated sim-land discipline is unenforced. For a bilingual school this is a material coverage hole.
proposed_resolution: Add a bilingual-measurement discipline as a generation contract + a per-spec in-execution self-check (mirroring the T3 coverage / T4 distribution / FGAP-037 three-view pattern): the measurement-bearing spec must produce a bilingual-progress measure (staff + student) or surface it as an explicit gap; gate it. A future fix task in the FGAP-033 in-execution-enforcement family.
